formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here's a comprehensive formula sheet for CBSE Class 11 Chemistry - Equilibrium in JSON format:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Law of Equilibrium",
    "formula": "At equilibrium, the rate of forward reaction = rate of backward reaction",
    "notes": "This law states that in a state of equilibrium, the rates of forward and reverse reactions are equal."
  },
  {
    "name": "Equilibrium Constant (Kc)",
    "formula": "Kc = [C]^c [D]^d / [A]^a [B]^b",
    "variables": {
      "Kc": "Equilibrium constant",
      "[C]": "Concentration of product C",
      "[D]": "Concentration of product D",
      "[A]": "Concentration of reactant A",
      "[B]": "Concentration of reactant B",
      "c": "Number of moles of product C",
      "d": "Number of moles of product D",
      "a": "Number of moles of reactant A",
      "b": "Number of moles of reactant B"
    },
    "units": "Unitless (concentrations in molarity)",
    "notes": "This formula is used to calculate the equilibrium constant (Kc) of a chemical reaction."
  },
  {
    "name": "Equilibrium Expression (Kp)",
    "formula": "Kp = (pC)^c (pD)^d / (pA)^a (pB)^b",
    "variables": {
      "Kp": "Equilibrium constant",
      "pC": "Partial pressure of product C",
      "pD": "Partial pressure of product D",
      "pA": "Partial pressure of reactant A",
      "pB": "Partial pressure of reactant B",
      "c": "Number of moles of product C",
      "d": "Number of moles of product D",
      "a": "Number of moles of reactant A",
      "b": "Number of moles of reactant B"
    },
    "units": "Unitless (pressures in atm)",
    "notes": "This formula is used to calculate the equilibrium constant (Kp) of a chemical reaction in terms of partial pressures."
  },
  {
    "name": "Henderson-Hasselbalch Equation",
    "formula": "pH = pKa + log10 (A-/HA)",
    "variables": {
      "pH": "Hydrogen ion concentration (in pH units)",
      "pKa": "Acid dissociation constant (in pH units)",
      "A-": "Concentration of conjugate base",
      "HA": "Concentration of weak acid"
    },
    "units": "pH units",
    "notes": "This formula is used to calculate the pH of a solution containing a weak acid and its conjugate base."
  },
  {
    "name": "van't Hoff Equation",
    "formula": "ΔG = ΔH - TΔS",
    "variables": {
      "ΔG": "Change in Gibbs free energy",
      "ΔH": "Change in enthalpy",
      "T": "Temperature (in Kelvin)",
      "ΔS": "Change in entropy"
    },
    "units": "J/mol",
    "notes": "This formula is used to calculate the change in Gibbs free energy (ΔG) of a system in terms of enthalpy (ΔH) and entropy (ΔS)."
  },
  {
    "name": "Law of Mass Action",
    "formula": "Rate = k[A]^a [B]^b",
    "variables": {
      "Rate": "Rate of reaction",
      "k": "Rate constant",
      "[A]": "Concentration of reactant A",
      "[B]": "Concentration of reactant B",
      "a": "Order of reaction with respect to A",
      "b": "Order of reaction with respect to B"
    },
    "units": "M^-1 s^-1 (concentrations in molarity)",
    "notes": "This formula is used to express the rate of reaction in terms of reactant concentrations and the rate constant."
  },
  {
    "name": "Rate Law",
    "formula": "Rate = k[T]^x [A]^a [B]^b",
    "variables": {
      "Rate": "Rate of reaction",
      "k": "Rate constant",
      "[T]": "Temperature (in Kelvin)",
      "[A]": "Concentration of reactant A",
      "[B]": "Concentration of reactant B",
      "x": "Order of reaction with respect to temperature",
      "a": "Order of reaction with respect to A",
      "b": "Order of reaction with respect to B"
    },
    "units": "M^-1 s^-1 (concentrations in molarity)",
    "notes": "This formula is used to express the rate of reaction in terms of temperature, reactant concentrations, and the rate constant."
  }
]
```

Please note that some of the formulas may have additional variables or conditions that are not included in the JSON object for brevity.
